name: Maynard Ferguson
abstract: Walter Maynard Ferguson CM (May 4, 1928 – August 23, 2006) was a Canadian jazz trumpeter and bandleader. He came to prominence in Stan Kenton's orchestra before forming his own big band in 1957.

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Erskine
about_quotes.evidence: Maynard was the best boss I ever had. ... I had turned it down three times I think when he called ... I had played with Kenton. Now they had both played with Maynard’s band way back in the start of both of their careers. ... You’ve played in the big bands of Stan Kenton and Maynard Ferguson
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Erskine interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 9, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Vince DiMartino
about_quotes.evidence: Try to play along with Maynard Ferguson and I’m going, “They don’t even have the fingerings in the book for this, so how am I supposed to do this?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince DiMartino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 8, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: So I started playing bari with Maynard. And that was a childhood dream.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Bergeron
about_quotes.evidence: I just pulled out my Maynard Ferguson tour jacket from 1986... I played on his final CD as a guest you know. He got me in and he did a tune on one of my CDs with me, six months before he passed away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Bergeron interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: And then I got close to Buddy Childers who played lead on the Kenton band, and I worked with Maynard, did a bunch of concerts with him, and Doc of course who is in a league by himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Then from that band, he went to Maynard’s band... Maynard did. Maynard was a very nice guy... and gave us the opportunity to try to develop our skills as arrangers and players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
